subject: physics
title: Temperature and Pressure at Constant Volume
summary: A short reference explaining why gas pressure rises when temperature increases at constant volume.
lecture_text: At constant volume, gas particles move faster as temperature increases. More frequent and stronger collisions with the container walls increase pressure.
visual_hints: Show a sealed container with faster particle motion.
Use arrows to connect temperature increase to pressure increase.

examples.question: What happens to pressure when temperature rises in a fixed-volume container?
